item.title: Google should allow third-party search engines access to data, EU says
item.description: The European Commission has sent preliminary findings вҖӢto Google on proposed вҖҢmeasures to comply with the EU's Digital Markets Act, which вҖӢwould allow third-party search вҖӢengines to access Google search вҒ data, including that of вҖӢartificial intelligence chatbots with search вҖӢfunctionalities, the commission said on Thursday.

item.title: Trump tries economic reset as Republicans fret over high gas prices
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump will look to brush off concerns about the economy and the sagging political prospects of his Republican Party during a campaign-style swing through the battleground вҖӢstates of Nevada and Arizona this week, as the war with Iran pushes gasoline prices higher.

item.title: Fed leadership transition on shaky ground as clock ticks on Powell's term
item.description: The prospect of a smooth-and-on-time вҖӢtransition to U.S. President Donald Trump's pick for the next Federal Reserve chief, Kevin Warsh, looks increasingly to be on shaky ground, setting up a possible clash over who runs things in вҖҢthe meantime.

item.title: US ESG shareholder resolutions plunge in face of Republican pressure, report says
item.description: Shareholders have filed 184 proposals promoting environmental, social and governance themes at U.S. companies so far this proxy season, a вҖӢnew study found, about half as many as last year, вҖҢas Republicans in Washington work to shift corporate power from investors to managers.

item.title: US will punish fraud, insider trading, derivatives regulator tells Congress
item.description: Washington's top derivatives regulator is due on Thursday to reassure lawmakers that the United States will punish fraud as concern mounts on Capitol Hill that oil, stock and вҖӢprediction market players are illicitly trading on inside information from the White House, вҖҢaccording to prepared remarks seen by Reuters.

item.title: Wall Street bank earnings in five charts as volatile markets take center stage
item.description: Major U.S. banks delivered a split picture this week, as Middle East-linked market volatility lifted trading in the first quarter but clouded dealmaking prospects.

item.title: Hedge funds on track for best monthly returns in over a decade, Goldman says
item.description: Hedge вҖҢfunds are on track for their best monthly performance in more than a decade, after overcoming a market downturn in March triggered by the Iran war that dragged returns lower, according вҖӢto Goldman Sachs' latest quarterly hedge fund industry report.

item.title: Exclusive: Starlink outage hit drone tests, exposing PentagonвҖҷs growing reliance on SpaceX
item.description: Last August, U.S. Navy officials carrying out a test of unmanned vessels realized they had hit a single point of failure: Starlink. A global outage across Elon MuskвҖҷs satellite network вҖӢaffecting millions of Starlink users had left two dozen unmanned surface vessels bobbing off the California coast, disrupting communications and halting operations for almost an hour.

item.title: PepsiCo tops quarterly revenue estimates as price cuts drive demand
item.description: PepsiCo beat analysts' estimates for quarterly revenue and stuck to its annual targets on Thursday, as recent price cuts for key snack вҖӢbrands helped revive demand in the U.S. and continued strength in its energy вҖҢdrinks and prebiotic sodas.

item.title: Relaxation of US day-trading rules opens door to YOLO trading, higher risk
item.description: A regulatory move allowing smaller, everyday investors to engage in more day trading could spur impulsive, high-risk "YOLO", or "you-only-live-once," trades вҖӢand allow eager individual traders to take an even bigger role in driving markets.

item.title: Boeing hiring more than 100 factory workers a week to grow output, replace retirees
item.description: Boeing вҖӢis hiring around 100 to 140 factory workers a week, the highest pace since 2024, as the U.S. вҖҢjetmaker replaces retirees and increases staffing to support higher production rates and new models, a union leader said.

item.title: German banks examine risks of Anthropic's Mythos with authorities
item.description: German banks and national authorities are examining risks around Anthropic's new artificial intelligence model, an official said on вҖӢThursday, amid concerns that it could fuel cyberattacks.

item.title: Wall St futures edge higher on Mideast diplomacy hopes, strong earnings
item.description: U.S. stock index futures inched higher on Thursday вҖӢas investors grew more confident that the worst of the Middle East conflict had passed, while a wave вҖҢof solid earnings allayed concerns about the economy.

item.title: Brazilian payments firm Ebanx makes Southeast Asia push
item.description: Brazilian cross-border payments company Ebanx said it is expanding global operations, with a focus on Southeast Asia, strengthening its footprint outside Latin вҖӢAmerica.

item.title: Pernod CFO says talks with Brown-Forman are "ongoing"
item.description: Pernod Ricard's talks to вҖӢmerge with U.S. вҖҢrival Brown-Forman are continuing, Pernod Ricard's вҖӢfinance chief said вҖӢon Thursday.

item.title: EQT restarts sale of contact lens maker Ginko in at least $1 billion deal, sources say
item.description: Private equity firm EQT is restarting the sale process for the mainland Chinese business of вҖӢcontact lens maker Ginko International, targeting a valuation of вҖҢat least $1 billion, three people with knowledge of the matter said.

item.title: Prince Harry praises Australia's leadership on social media ban as Meghan tells of online bullying
item.description: Britain's Prince Harry on Thursday praised Australia's "epic" leadership on curbing harmful social вҖҢmedia use for teens, as his wife Meghan, Duchess of Sussex, spoke of a decade of online abuse.

item.title: Zara owner Inditex reports unauthorised access to transaction databases
item.description: Zara owner Inditex said it had вҖӢidentified unauthorised access вҖҢto databases hosted by a third party containing вҖӢinformation on transactions вҖӢwith customers.

item.title: Samsung asks court to block labour unions from holding a strike
item.description: Samsung Electronics asked a вҖӢcourt to block вҖҢits South Korean labour unions from вҖӢholding a strike, вҖӢMoneyToday reported on Thursday.

item.title: Circle CEO sees 'tremendous opportunity' for yuan-backed stablecoin
item.description: Circle Internet Group sees "tremendous opportunity" for yuan-backed stablecoin as digital money becomes increasingly integrated into global trade and finance, said the chief of the world's largest regulated stablecoin issuer.

item.title: Hyundai Motor's US sales chief has left the company, Automotive News reports
item.description: Michael вҖӢOrange, the вҖҢHyundai Motor вҖӢexecutive вҖӢresponsible for sales вҒ in вҖӢall of вҖӢthe automaker's seven вҖӢU.S. вҖӢregions, left the вҖҢcompany вҒ on Tuesday, Automotive News вҖӢreported.

item.title: TSMC Q1 profit jumps 58% to record, beats expectations
item.description: TSMC, the world's largest contract вҖӢchipmaker, posted a вҖҢ58% jump in first-quarter net profit on Thursday, вҖӢbeating market forecasts вҖӢand hitting a record, вҒ as it benefits вҖӢfrom huge appetite globally вҖӢfor its artificial intelligence processors.

item.title: Pernod Ricard warns Iran war to hit full-year sales despite Q3 rebound
item.description: Pernod Ricard , which is in talks to merge with U.S. rival Brown-Forman , reported a stronger-than-expected 0.1% rise in sales for the third quarter but warned вҖӢthe decline in tourism due to the war in Iran would hurt its вҖҢtravel retail business and impact full-year sales.

item.title: TomTom's reorganization boosts operating profit in Q1
item.description: Dutch digital mapping specialist TomTom вҖҢreported on Thursday higher than expected operating profit for the first-quarter of the year вҖӢdespite lower revenue following an вҖӢinternal reorganization.

item.title: Morning Bid: Truce trades ramp up as Trump touts Israel-Lebanon talks
item.description: A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Gregor Stuart Hunter

item.title: BOJ to hike rates by June as war-fuelled inflation risks mount: Reuters poll
item.description: The Bank of Japan will raise its benchmark rate to 1.00% by end-June, with nearly two-thirds of economists in a Reuters poll predicting the move, and a hike this month or in June seen as вҖӢequally likely amid uncertainty over the fallout from the Iran war.

item.title: European banks can withstand current shocks, watchdog head says
item.description: European lenders are resilient enough to absorb current financial and geopolitical вҖҢshocks but need to prepare for future uncertainties including cybersecurity risks from AI, FranГ§ois-Louis Michaud, the new head of the European Banking Authority, said.

item.title: Musk's staff reaches out to suppliers for Terafab project, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: Elon Musk's staff have reached out to chip industry вҖӢsuppliers for his Terafab AI chip complex вҖҢproject with SpaceX and Tesla , Bloomberg News reported on Wednesday.

item.title: Iran war brings US close to net crude exporter for first time since World War Two
item.description: The U.S. nearly turned into a net crude exporter last week for the first time since World War Two вҖӢas shipments surged close to a record high to meet demand from Asian and European buyers scrambling to replace Middle East supplies cut by вҖҢthe Iran war.

item.title: Australia's jobless rate stays low in March but Iran war risks loom
item.description: Australian employment rose broadly in line with expectations in March, while the jobless rate stayed low, but a war-driven вҖӢglobal energy shock could test the labour market's resilience soon.
